name: Spanish Tomato Rice
steps: Sauté chopped onions, garlic, and capsicum in olive oil until soft.
Add diced tomatoes, paprika, and cook for 5 minutes until tomatoes break down.
Stir in the rice, coating it with the tomato mixture.
Add water or broth, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 15-20 minutes until rice is cooked.

name: Korean Egg Fried Rice
steps: Beat eggs in a bowl with a pinch of salt.
Heat a pan with oil, scramble the eggs until 80% cooked, then remove and set aside.
Sauté chopped green onions, onions, and garlic in the same pan.
Add cold cooked rice and stir-fry, breaking up any clumps.
Drizzle soy sauce around the edges of the pan, stir-fry to combine.
Return the scrambled eggs to the pan, toss together, and finish with sesame oil.

name: Chinese Tomato Egg Rice
steps: Beat eggs and scramble in a hot pan with oil until soft-curd, then remove.
In the same pan, cook chopped garlic and green onions, then add wedged tomatoes.
Cook tomatoes down until they form a juicy sauce, adding sugar and soy sauce.
Add the eggs back into the tomato sauce, mixing gently.
Serve the hot tomato-egg stir fry over a bed of warm steamed rice.

name: Indian Egg Bhurji Rice
steps: Sauté finely chopped onion, garlic, and green chili in oil until light brown.
Add chopped capsicum and tomatoes, cooking until soft.
Stir in turmeric, cumin, and coriander powder.
Whisk eggs and pour them directly into the pan, scrambling on medium heat.
Fold in cooked rice, mix well until heated through, and garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Italian Risotto al Pomodoro
steps: Heat olive oil and butter, and sauté finely chopped onion and garlic.
Add rice and toast for 2 minutes until translucent.
Slowly add pureed tomatoes and warm broth, one ladle at a time, stirring constantly.
Allow the rice to absorb liquid before adding more, cooking for 18 minutes.
Stir in parmesan, fresh basil, and a touch of butter for a creamy finish.

name: Mexican Tomato Salsa & Rice Bowl
steps: Cook rice with tomato paste, garlic, and cumin.
Make fresh salsa: dice tomatoes, onions, capsicum, and coriander, then mix with lime juice and salt.
Sauté black beans or extra capsicum if desired.
Assemble the bowl with a base of Mexican rice, topped with fresh tomato salsa.

name: Spanish Tortilla
steps: Peel and thinly slice potatoes and onions.
Gently fry potatoes and onions in a generous amount of olive oil until soft, not browned.
Drain the potatoes and onions and mix them with beaten eggs in a bowl.
Pour the mixture back into a hot pan with a little oil and cook until the bottom sets.
Carefully flip using a plate, and cook the other side until golden and cooked through.

name: Japanese Tamago Kake Gohan
steps: Prepare a bowl of steaming hot freshly cooked rice.
Crack a fresh, high-quality egg directly over the hot rice.
Add a splash of soy sauce and a few drops of sesame oil.
Whip vigorously with chopsticks until the egg becomes frothy and coats every grain of rice.
Garnish with chopped green onions.

name: Italian Tomato Bruschetta
steps: Dice tomatoes and mix with minced garlic, chopped basil, olive oil, and salt. Let sit to combine flavors.
Slice Italian bread and grill or toast until crunchy.
Rub a cut garlic clove over the toasted bread slices.
Spoon the marinated tomato mixture generously over the toasted bread.

name: Indian Jeera Rice
steps: Wash and soak basmati rice for 20 minutes.
Heat ghee (or oil) in a pot, add cumin seeds, and let them sizzle.
Add sliced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until soft.
Add the drained rice and sauté for 1 minute.
Add water and salt, bring to a boil, then cover and cook on low heat for 12 minutes.

name: Middle Eastern Shakshuka
steps: Sauté chopped onions, garlic, and capsicum in olive oil in a skillet until soft.
Add diced tomatoes, cumin, paprika, salt, and cook until it forms a thick sauce (about 10 minutes).
Use a spoon to make small wells in the sauce and crack eggs directly into them.
Cover the skillet and cook on low-medium heat for 5-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are runny.

name: Classic Tomato Omelette
steps: Whisk eggs with salt and pepper in a bowl.
Finely chop tomato, onion, and capsicum.
Melt butter in a pan, pour in the eggs, and cook until they start to set.
Sprinkle the chopped vegetables over one half of the omelette.
Fold the omelette in half and cook for 1 more minute before serving.

name: Sautéed Garlic Tomatoes
steps: Halve cherry tomatoes or thick-slice regular tomatoes.
Sauté sliced garlic in olive oil on medium heat until fragrant (do not burn).
Add the tomatoes, toss to coat, and cook for 3-5 minutes until soft and blistered.
Season with salt and fresh cracked pepper, and serve warm.

name: Garlic Butter Potato Hash
steps: Peel potatoes and cut into small 1/2-inch cubes.
Melt butter in a skillet, add chopped onion and potato cubes, and cook on medium-high.
Stir occasionally, letting the potato edges turn golden and crispy (about 15 minutes).
Add minced garlic in the final 3 minutes of cooking, tossing well.
Season with salt and black pepper.
